description: Freddy Malick and the Chronicom hunters confront Coulson and May, using Nathaniel Malick as leverage. Daisy takes Nathaniel hostage, allowing the S.H.I.E.L.D. agents a narrow escape. As tension builds, the Chronicoms pursue them, but Enoch arrives in a car at the last moment to help the team get away, providing a brief respite and critical support.
location: Koenig's speakeasy (Ernest Koenig's bar / S.H.I.E.L.D. safe house)

transcript.line: Hey. Just wanted to thank you for coming in tonight
Pleasure. It's an honor to serve S.H.I.E.L.D.
I bet it is. Uh... sorry, what's your name again?
Patrick. Patrick Kutik.
Right. And remind me, where did you come from?
Uh, processing. Systems Analysis. It's a little bit of quantitative. Some days, a lot of qualitative.
Have you ever been up to River's End? I just bought a house in town.
I can't say that I have.
Yeah? My friends told me differently. Did you really think I wouldn't remember you?
Those are Chronicoms? They look...
Subtle, I know.
So, what's our play?
We first met years ago, in this very room. I've lived an entire lifetime since then. What have you been doing?
You should've taken our offer when you had the chance, Mr. Coulson. Surrender now, and we'll go easy on the rest of you.
Counter offer. Let us go, and the kid walks.
You wouldn't. Nathaniel's got nothing to do with this.
He's a ripple. Doesn't belong here.
I'd hate to do something we'd all regret. So what's it gonna be?
Enough. This ends now.
Stand down. Stand down!
Sorry, kid. It's nothing personal.
This is strange behavior.
I had no choice. They have my son.
Get them.
So S.H.I.E.L.D. takes hostages now? Anything else you aren't telling me?
I'll explain later. Let's go.
We got to find some wheels and get out of here.
Come with me if you want to continue to exist!
Enoch, you're here.
Yes. As I have been for the last years. Now buckle up.
Sorry we left you behind, thanks for the lift, and sweet ride.
Thank you. Consumer Reports gave this vehicle a five-star safety rating, and its fuel economy is best in class.
It's time we change the rules.
